title: Future of YouTube isn't highly produced videos but self graded intelligence & strength tests

enrichment.summary: Kyle predicts YouTube will hit a saturation point with heavily produced, clout-driven content and will evolve back toward authentic self-expression. He proposes a standardization system where people use YouTube to demonstrate real knowledge through self-graded IQ tests and a 'bi-weekly history test' format. He weaves in critiques of soldier/criminal influencer culture, parallels to the fall of Rome, a broken educational system, and his own experiences with job rejection and online harassment.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Will return to authentic self-broadcast once clout-chasing saturates; needs cultural standardization not law
Spreading a harmful, violent philosophy that mirrors Rome's militarization and societal decline
Skeptical when they still threaten violence; calls it fake Christianity for clout
Childish and dangerous; follower count should not confer authority or political candidacy

stances.topic: Self-graded IQ and history tests on YouTube
stances.position: Genuinely good way to demonstrate intelligence transparently and create real meritocracy
Designed for industrial-era compliance, not actual learning; grades are arbitrary and punitive
Against it; wears low view counts as a badge of honor; believes videos should be a civic/personal act
Credentials are becoming smoke and mirrors; credential inflation crowds out genuine merit

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is the prophet of prophets because he found Atlantis.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: YouTube will hit a saturation point where everyone is watching it all the time.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: YouTube will evolve to go back to its original format where people do videos because they want to, not for fame.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: If storage becomes an issue, YouTube could prioritize educational videos over silly ones.

claims.subject: social media
claims.claim: Social media is wreaking havoc on young girls because it turns into a competition.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: People are watching YouTube everywhere, even in the gym.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: In the past, people would quit YouTube if they didn't make money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He started uploading YouTube videos in 2009 because he finally got a camera.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: Celebrities initially lagged in joining YouTube, possibly due to harassment concerns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes everyone has a platform to defend themselves on YouTube, even against slander.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: There has been an explosion of new creators in recent years, leading to oversaturation.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: Warrior influencers (e.g., Navy SEALs) are becoming oversaturated, making it hard for new ones to succeed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wears low view counts as a badge of honor because he believes people should spend time on better things.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: In the future, people will upload videos just for the sake of it, not for hits.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: YouTube should be standardized like railroad tracks to create a meritocracy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to upload IQ test videos to motivate himself to improve.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He faced online harassment after getting a DUI for marijuana in 2015.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His website improved over time, reducing harassment against him.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: The future of YouTube should involve self-education, like book reviews and history tests.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He proposed a 'bi-weekly history test' where people test their knowledge of random decades.

claims.subject: education system
claims.claim: The U.S. education system is designed to brainwash people rather than make them smart.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He struggled in college due to unfair grading and authority figures disliking him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes the future of YouTube should be about transparency and self-improvement, not popularity.
